latest.title: Change of name and TIDM
latest.content:
Today 10:49
RNS Number : 5289O
Cykel AI PLC
12 January 2026
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
12 January 2026
DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC (formerly Cykel AI PLC)
(the "
Company
")
Change of name and TIDM
DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC (LSE: DFDV), a Main Market listed company which offers an AI agent platform that enables businesses to automate tasks through the use of specialised digital workers for
sales, recruitment and research and has adopted
a Solana focused digital asset treasury strategy,
is pleased to announce that the Company has changed its name and will trade under its new name DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC with effect from 12 January 2026.
In addition, with effect from 13 January 2026, the Company's TIDM will be amended from "CYK" to "DFDV" on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange.
The decision to change name to
DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC
reflects the Company's continued development of its Solana focused digital asset treasury strategy as previously announced on 28 August 2025. The "Cykel" trading name will continue to be used for the Company's AI agent activities.
In addition, the Company's website address will also change to www.defidevcorp.co.uk.
Shareholders should note that their shareholdings will be unaffected by the change of name. Existing share certificates should be retained as they will remain valid for all purposes and no new share certificates will be issued.
The Company's ISIN and SEDOL will remain unchanged. The Company's Legal Entity Identifier remains 213800QEO6L6JAS62H02.
The Directors of the Company accept responsibility for this announcement.
*ENDS*
For further information:
Company
Michael Chan
Director
investors@defidevcorp.co.uk
First Sentinel (Corporate Adviser)

latest.title: Further re warrants
latest.content:
4 Dec 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 1455K
Cykel AI PLC
04 December 2025
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
4 December 2025
Cykel AI PLC
("
Cykel AI
" or the "
Company
")
Further re Warrants
Cykel AI PLC (LSE:CYK) announces the following with respect to the warrants, full details of which are set out in the Company's announcement of 28 August 2025.
Warrants cancellation
The Company announces that it has agreed to the cancellation of 5,000,000 Pre-Paid and Investor Warrants (together, the "Warrants"). This will result in the repayment of the deposit already paid for the Pre-Paid Warrants (less a contribution to transaction costs) to the cancelling holders in accordance with the terms of the Warrants. Following this cancellation, the Company has 41,666,667 Warrants outstanding.
Warrants extension details
Under the existing terms, Warrants are not capable of being exercised until certain conditions which are detailed in the 28 August 2025 announcement have been satisfied and would have expired if such conditions were not satisfied by 30 November 2025. The Company and the remaining warrant holders, including DeFi Development Corp, have agreed to extend the period within which these conditions may be satisfied to 1 June 2026. Accordingly, the Warrants will only become exercisable once the relevant conditions have been fulfilled on or before 1 June 2026.
If the conditions have not been satisfied by 1 June 2026, the Warrants will expire and be cancelled and the deposit for the Pre-Paid Warrants will be repaid to the relevant investors (less pre
‑
agreed contributions to transaction costs).
All other terms of the Warrants remain unchanged.
Pre-Paid Warrants and Investor Warrants have the meanings given to them in the announcement published on 28 August 2025.
For further information:
Cykel AI plc
Michael Chan
CEO

latest.title: Changes in Board Composition
latest.content:
3 Nov 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 7828F
Cykel AI PLC
03 November 2025
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
3 November 2025
Cykel AI PLC
("Cykel AI" or the "Company")
Changes in Board Composition
Cykel AI PLC (LSE:CYK) announces that Nicholas Lyth has resigned as Interim Chairman of the Company, with immediate effect.
The Board would like to take this opportunity to thank Mr. Lyth for his contributions to the Company and wish him well for the future.
Michael Chan will assume the role of Chairman on an interim basis pending the appointment of a Non-executive Chairman in due course.
For further information:
Cykel AI plc
Michael Chan
CEO
investors@cykel.ai
First Sentinel (Financial Adviser)

company_30s.what_they_do: Cykel AI builds AI‑driven sales and marketing tools. Its flagship products, Eve and GTM AI, automate lead qualification, pricing, and campaign management for niche verticals such as cycling and outdoor gear.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company tackles a $2 billion global AI‑sales‑automation market, offering a plug‑and‑play platform that can reduce marketing spend by 30‑40% and accelerate revenue cycles for mid‑size brands.
company_30s.current_state: Cykel AI is still pre‑revenue, having raised several funding rounds to shore up cash. It has re‑branded, shuffled its board, and announced new AI products, but its share price sits at a fraction of its former valuation, reflecting a steep discount after a prolonged slump.

rns_narrative: The company is pivoting from a generic tech start‑up to a niche AI platform, as shown by the launch of Eve and GTM AI. This signals a strategic shift toward higher‑margin B2B services.
Cykel AI has been aggressively raising capital – a £2.8 m raise, a £750 k placement, and a Bitcoin treasury reserve strategy – indicating a focus on liquidity and long‑term resilience.
Board changes and a name change suggest leadership is aligning the company’s vision with its new product roadmap, creating a narrative of renewed purpose.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet hope – the community is largely silent, with a handful of bullish chatter that feels like a whisper.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders see the AI launches and Bitcoin reserve as a signal that the company has found a defensible moat and will finally turn a profit.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others point to the repeated capital raises, lack of revenue, and the volatility of the AI sector as evidence that the business model is still unproven and the stock is over‑discounted.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Over the past week, sentiment has moved from cautious optimism to a cautious wait‑and‑see stance, as the market absorbs the new product announcements but remains skeptical about immediate upside.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no recent spikes in search interest, which means a catalyst could surprise the market.

near_term_catalysts: A Q2 earnings report that shows a positive cash burn rate and a clear revenue pipeline.
Positive commentary from a major cycling retailer that adopts Eve for its online sales.
A spike in trading volume that breaks the 20‑day moving average support level, indicating renewed institutional interest.
A formal announcement of a partnership with a large e‑commerce platform, expanding the product’s reach.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained volume uptick, a break above the 10‑day low, and any sign that the company’s revenue projections have improved. A sudden drop in the Bitcoin reserve or a regulatory warning would be a red flag.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early recovery phase of a deep crash. It has not yet found a clear support base, so a confirmed bounce would likely come after a catalyst that validates the new AI roadmap.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The signal classification aligns with a deep crash bottom, which historically yields 40‑50% upside. If the price recovers to halfway between its recent low and its 52‑week high, that’s a 100%+ upside. The Bitcoin treasury adds a layer of liquidity that can cushion any short‑term volatility.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still lacks revenue and has a history of capital raises to stay afloat. If the AI products fail to generate traction or if a major partnership falls through, the stock could stay below its current level for an extended period.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the current price because the stock is already heavily discounted. Upside is asymmetric – a 200% rebound to the 52‑week high would more than double any investment.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a bounce off the 10‑day low, size 10% of your discretionary capital, and place a stop just below that low. Use a 3‑to‑1 reward:stop ratio to protect against a temporary pullback.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash or a small position until you see volume exceed 1.5× the 20‑day average and the price breaks the 10‑day low with a 3‑minute candle. That’s a clear confirmation of a mean‑reversion move.
playbook.if_passing: None – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy signals. The only terminal risk is that the AI products fail to gain traction.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Cykel AI sits on a deep crash, but the recent AI launches and Bitcoin reserve strategy create a plausible catalyst that could trigger a significant bounce, offering a highly asymmetric upside if the market validates the new product roadmap.
